name: Perimeter — The Walk Around the Edge
whatIsIt: Perimeter is the total distance AROUND a shape — the length of a full walk along its boundary. Find it by adding ALL the side lengths. For a rectangle there's a shortcut: 2 × (length + breadth), because opposite sides come in equal pairs.
whyNeeded: Fencing a garden, framing a photo, putting ribbon around a gift, lining a playground — whenever something goes AROUND an edge, you're buying perimeter! Get it wrong and your fence stops halfway.
realLifeUses: Fencing: how much wire fence for the vegetable garden?
Craft: ribbon around a greeting card's border
Sports: one lap around the playground boundary
Sewing: lace around the edge of a tablecloth

vocabulary.meaning: The total distance around a shape's boundary. Peri = around!
The edge line of a shape — where the walk happens.
A rectangle's long side and short side.

story.title: The March Around the Tulsi Garden
story.text: Ajji's rectangular tulsi garden is 5 m long and 3 m wide. 'If you march all the way around the edge, how far is that?' asks Ajji. Meena walks the four sides: 5 + 3 + 5 + 3. 'Sixteen metres!' 'Or the shortcut: 2 × (5 + 3) = 16. The perimeter is the whole walk around the boundary,' nods Ajji.
story.extractedProblem: A rectangular garden is 5 m long and 3 m wide. How far is one full march around it?
story.answerInStory: 16 m — all four sides: 5+3+5+3, or the shortcut 2×(5+3).

visual.caption: Walk the whole boundary: two 5s and two 3s. Perimeter = 2 × (5 + 3) = 16.

teachingGallery.title: Perimeter — the walk around the edge
teachingGallery.note: Add up every side. That total is the perimeter.

standardMethod.summary: Add ALL the sides — every single one of the walk.
steps: List every side length of the shape.
Answer with UNITS: 16 metres (a walk is measured in length units!).

alternateMethods.name: Finger-walk method
steps: Put your finger on one corner and 'walk' the boundary.
Say each side's length as you pass it.
Stop when you're back at the start — add what you said.

alternateMethods.name: Square shortcut
alternateMethods.whenToUse: All sides equal? Multiply instead of adding!

workedExamples.problem: A square photo frame has sides of 6 cm. How much ribbon goes around it?
steps: Answer with units: 24 cm of ribbon.

workedExamples.problem: Ravi fenced a 8 m by 4 m rectangle with 12 m of wire — 'because 8 + 4 = 12'. Why is his garden half-open?
steps: 8 + 4 covers only TWO sides — one length and one breadth.
True perimeter: 24 m. Ravi is 12 m of fence short — the goats are delighted.

trickPractice.intro: Perimeter is just the fence around the shape — total up all the sides.

questions.q: A square has side 5. What is its perimeter?
A square has side 4. What is its perimeter?
A square has side 7. What is its perimeter?
A square has side 6. What is its perimeter?
A rectangle is 6 long and 3 wide. Perimeter?
A rectangle is 8 long and 2 wide. Perimeter?
A rectangle is 5 long and 4 wide. Perimeter?
A rectangle is 7 long and 3 wide. Perimeter?

commonMistakes.mistake: Adding just length + breadth (5+3=8) instead of the whole way around.
commonMistakes.fix: Finger-walk the boundary! You pass FOUR sides on a rectangle — two lengths and two breadths. 2×(5+3), never just 5+3.

commonMistakes.mistake: Multiplying length × breadth (5×3=15) — that's a different measurement entirely!
commonMistakes.fix: Multiplying sides gives AREA (the space inside — next adventure!). Perimeter is a WALK, and walks are ADDED, side by side.

commonMistakes.mistake: Answering '16' with no units, or with square units.
commonMistakes.fix: Perimeter is a length — metres, centimetres. Say the units every time: 16 metres of marching!

easy.q: A rectangle is 6 m long and 2 m wide. What is its perimeter in metres?
hintLadder: Finger-walk: how many sides do you pass?

easy.q: A square carrom board has 4 sides of 7 dm each. Perimeter in dm?
hintLadder: All sides equal — use the square shortcut.

medium.q: A triangle park has sides 40 m, 30 m, and 50 m. How long is one lap around it, in metres?
For a 5 × 3 rectangle, Ravi computed the 'perimeter' as 15. What did he actually compute?

options.label: He multiplied the sides — that's the area, not the perimeter

medium.answer: He multiplied the sides — that's the area, not the perimeter
hintLadder: Multiplying sides measures the INSIDE space.

challenge.q: A rectangular kitchen garden is 12 m by 8 m. Fencing costs are per metre, so first: how many metres of fence are needed?
Reverse puzzle! A square field has a perimeter of 36 m. How long is ONE side, in metres?

questions.q: Perimeter of a 7 m by 3 m rectangle, in metres?

options.label: The total distance around the boundary

questions.answer: The total distance around the boundary

questions.q: A square has sides of 5 cm. Perimeter in cm?
For a rectangle, adding only length + breadth gives…

options.label: Half the perimeter — the walk has four sides!

questions.answer: Half the perimeter — the walk has four sides!
hintLadder: How many sides did you pass?

teachBackPrompt: Lead your own ant parade! Pick any table or book at home, finger-walk its boundary saying each side aloud, and explain to someone why Ravi's 'just add two sides' fence left the goats a doorway.

revisionCard.summary: Perimeter = the walk AROUND = add ALL sides. Rectangle: 2×(l+b). Square: 4×side. Never multiply the sides (that's area!) and never forget: two lengths AND two breadths. Always give length units!

gameMission.title: The Great Fence Contract
gameMission.brief: Shape City's gardens all need fencing before the goat festival! Measure each boundary walk, order exactly the right fence length, and audit Ravi's contracts — half his fences mysteriously stop midway.

realLifeProject: Fence your bed! Measure its length and breadth with a scale or measuring tape (parent's help), compute the perimeter both ways (add-all and shortcut), then check by walking a string around it.

rememberIt.hook: Perimeter = the fence AROUND (add all the sides)
rememberIt.unpack: 'Peri' = around, 'meter' = measure. Rectangle = 2(l + b). Square = 4 x side. It is a LENGTH (cm, m), not an area.

studentQuestions.q: Perimeter or area - what is the difference?
studentQuestions.a: Perimeter is the distance AROUND the edge (the fence). Area is the space INSIDE (the grass). Perimeter uses cm/m; area uses square units.
Add length and breadth, then double: 2(l + b). You have two of each side.
